subject: Sociology
title: The Myth of Meritocracy
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Myth of Meritocracy usable.

hook.text: She worked three jobs, got straight A's, and still couldn't afford college. Meritocracy promises what it can't deliver.

buildup.text: Meritocracy says talent and effort determine success. It's the foundational belief of most modern societies.

discovery.text: Research shows parental income predicts a child's earnings better than grades, test scores, or work ethic.

twist.text: Believing in meritocracy makes people harsher toward the poor. If success is earned, failure must be deserved.

climax.text: Meritocracy isn't just inaccurate — it's harmful. It turns structural failure into personal shame.

punchline.text: Meritocracy works best for those who already have advantages.

quiz.question: What do studies show about meritocracy?
options: Parental income predicts success better than talent or effort
Hard work is the only factor in success
